design_philosophy.core_loop: Create genetic variants of plants and animals, place them in custom ecosystems, observe evolutionary outcomes, iterate on genetic designs to achieve sustainability
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balance between creating novel life forms and ensuring they can survive self-sustaining ecosystems; tension between player agency and biological constraints
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing God with genetics—designing custom species and watching evolution unfold in a controlled laboratory environment; bridging entertainment with scientific literacy
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Frames genetic engineering and evolution as a playable, experimental sandbox rather than a narrative or competitive experience. Emphasizes emergent complexity from simple genetic rules. Educational framing ('bridges gap between entertainment and education') positions learning as primary value, not secondary

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Players control all variables: can create custom worlds with 'distinctive environments', modify physics of the universe, adjust ecosystem parameters, and design species genetics. No inherent win/loss conditions documented—difficulty emerges from player-imposed goals (e.g., achieving self-sustaining ecosystem)

progression.upgrade_system: Genetic modification system—players unlock new trait combinations and species variants by experimenting with genetics; evolution of created species as primary progression mechanic
progression.unlock_mechanism: No traditional unlock gates. Player agency determines progression; new species and ecosystems become available through experimentation and genetic design
replayability_hooks: Different environmental parameters to test species against
Creation of multiple unique species and monitoring their interactions
Testing Earth species vs. custom-designed species in various environments
Emergent complexity from genetic rule interactions

world_building_techniques: Environmental variety—'Earth's various environments' serve as context for species survival testing
Genetic/biological storytelling—species behavior emerges from genetic code rather than scripted narrative
Exclusive fictional creatures (Killer Penguin, Monkeyphant, Orgot) provide thematic flavor without narrative structure
Scientific framing positions ecosystem as 'virtual world' to study genetics in action

danger_signals: Uncontrolled species evolution creating ecosystem collapse
Overpowered player-designed predators eliminating all prey
Environmental parameters set to hostile extremes preventing survival
environmental_storytelling: Ecosystem health visible through species population dynamics
Environmental destruction visible as extinct species disappear
Successful symbiosis visible through stable multi-species populations
Player modifications to world physics manifested in observable environmental changes
